lines.speaker: Messenger
lines.text: Hello there. I have a message for you.

lines.speaker: Messenger
lines.text: Hello there. I have a message for you, but you'll need to make some room for it.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hey! You made it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I came as soon as I could. What are we dealing with?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Up here? Not a lot. The Devourer must be deeper inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well then I guess that's where we're going as well.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: We shouldn't leave this entry unguarded. The Devourer still has plenty of servants out in the desert who we won't want coming in after us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... fair point. Alright, you keep an eye on things up here while I go after Amascut.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I had a feeling you might say that, so I made some preparations.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Preparations?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Indeed. The Devourer has made plenty of enemies, and there are others willing to help us. Before you head deeper into the tomb, you might want to consider grouping up with some of them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that. Anything else I should know?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: It seems Osmumten had various magical protections built into his tomb. It looks like the Devourer disabled them, which should make things easier for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'd better not waste any more time. Amascut may well have already found the two weapons. If so, she'll already be draining the lesser gods of their power.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Meaning the longer we wait, the stronger she gets. If we don't stop her attaining true godhood, who knows what will happen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll stop her.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Oh, I almost forgot, I found this journal that might be worth a read.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Just let me know if you want it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. I'd better get ready.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to be doing?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: The Devourer has all four of the lesser gods somewhere in this tomb. We have to assume she's already found the two weapons she seeks as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Which means she'll already be draining the lesser gods of their power.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Exactly. You need to head deeper into the tomb, free the lesser gods and stop the Devourer before it's too late.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: This won't be an easy fight, so make sure you're well prepared. Thankfully, the Devourer is not short of enemies. If needs be, you can use the obelisk here and find some allies to join you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might just do that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have that journal from earlier?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I do, but you'll need to make some room for it.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I do. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Don't you already have it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so I do. Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Good luck.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: You did it! I wasn't sure I'd make it out of that one, but I'm glad you proved me wrong. Come, let's get out of here.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Amascut's hold on me is finally at an end. Human, let us return to the Nexus.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Even with everything we're going through, I have to admit, that was an impressive fight to behold! I wish I could have joined you, friend. Now, let us return to the Nexus.

lines.speaker: Scabaras
lines.text: The... the noise! Her voice in my head for so long, and now... silence! It's so loud! Please, we must leave!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there. How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Oh, you know, just getting over years of being controlled by a deranged family member. All part of the fun in a family like ours, I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You seem quite jolly about it all.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Well as I always say, 'the real winner is the one smiling.' What Amascut has done is horrific, but it's not too late to stop her. I won't let her get me down, not while there's still hope.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Anyway, I'm annoyingly a bit too weak to help you much down here. However, I reckon if you can free the others, we'll be able to open the way to the lower level for you.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Anyway, we've been able to open the way for you! I wish we could help you down there, but we're still too weak. Don't worry though, I know you can do it!

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: I'm annoyingly a bit too weak to help you much down here. However, I reckon if you can free the others, we'll be able to open the way to the lower level for you.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: We've been able to open the way for you! I wish we could help you down there, but we're still too weak. Don't worry though, I know you can do it!

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not feeling talkative?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: I'm sure you will find a conversation with me more appealing than one with Scabaras. Still, I leave most of the talking to Apmeken.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Now, I can't support you in your fight, but if you can free the others, we'll be able to open the way to Amascut.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Now, we've opened the way to Amascut, but we can do no more. From here, it's up to you.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Human, I can't support you in your fight, but if you can free the others, we'll be able to open the way to Amascut.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Human, we've opened the way to Amascut, but we can do no more. From here, it's up to you.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Amascut! She's always been a monster, but she's doing things now I never believed her to be capable of! Had I not let my guard down, she'd surely be regretting her choices by now.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: I guess we're lucky you came along when you did, my friend. I think you might have just given us a fighting chance!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm glad to have helped, but it's not over yet.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Indeed. Were I not still weak, I would gladly deal with her myself, but I fear you will have to face Amascut without me.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: You'll find her on the level below. If you can free the others, we should be able to open the way for you.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: You'll find her on the level below. We have managed to open the way for you. Go now, and show her the error of her ways!

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: My friend, Amascut has heavily weakened me, but I know you can do what is needed. If you can free the others, we should be able to open the way to the level below.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: We have opened the way for you, my friend. Any other day, I would have joined you in the fight against Amascut, but this one is up to you. Go now, and show her the error of her ways!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Scabaras
lines.text: The silence... it's so loud! Free the others! Free them and we can help. Now please... go!

lines.speaker: Scabaras
lines.text: We... opened the way. Now... go. Stop her! Please!

lines.speaker: Scabaras
lines.text: The silence... it's so loud! Free the others! Free them and we can help. Now please... go!

lines.speaker: Scabaras
lines.text: We... opened the way. Now... go. Stop her! Please!

lines.speaker: Amascut
lines.text: You! I should have known this would be your doing!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's over, Amascut! Your plan has failed.

lines.speaker: Amascut
lines.text: My plan has only just begun! Even with this setback, those pathetic fragments of my father have still given me ample power. My strength is absolute!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe, but it doesn't matter. I've stopped every one of your schemes so far, and I'll stop the next one as well.

lines.speaker: Amascut
lines.text: Really? You think you're leaving this place alive? I can promise you that won't be the case! My father's weapons have given me his power, and now they'll finally rid me of you!

lines.speaker: Osmumten
lines.text: The Devourer's thralls will be back, but they have been weakened because of you. Your prize awaits.

lines.speaker: Osmumten
lines.text: You have done well, but it is time for you to leave. The Devourer's thralls will soon return.

lines.speaker: Osmumten
lines.text: You fought well. The Devourer's influence here has lessened, but it will soon be restored. It is time for you to leave.

lines.speaker: Osmumten
lines.text: The Devourer's thralls will be back, but they have been weakened because of you. Now, leave this place.

lines.speaker: Spirit
lines.text: You. You fought against the Devourer. Her influence here has been lessened as a result. You are to be commended for your strength.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I take it you're Osmumten?

lines.speaker: Osmumten
lines.text: Once, but who I was in life means nothing. Vengeance is all that matters now. The Devourer may be gone, but my home has been corrupted. This corruption must be destroyed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help?

lines.speaker: Osmumten
lines.text: You can. Take your prize and leave, but when you are ready, return here. The Devourer's thralls will soon be back. You must fight them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll do what I can.

lines.speaker: Osmumten
lines.text: We shall see if it is enough.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the raid again before talking to the Avatars of Tumeken

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: My friend, we would like to have a word with you before you head back inside.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa before the Avatars of Tumeken depart

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: So this is a new one. There's a bunch of lesser gods standing just over there. You should probably go and talk to them.

sections.title: Talking to the Avatars of Tumeken
Talking to them again prior to their departure

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: You're back!

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: If you have questions, now is the time to ask them.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Here [he is/she is/they are]!

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: My friend, you managed what few have ever achieved. You overcame Amascut herself! I wish I could have been there to see it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I couldn't have done it alone. Speaking of which, where's Scabaras?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Scabaras walks his own path. Do not consider his absence an insult. This is his way.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Not to mention that he was under Amascut's control far longer than the rest of us. It will take him time to come to terms with his role in her plots.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Don't worry, he'll be okay. He always is.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Before we go any further... We didn't have much time for conversation back in the tomb. I'm sure you have some questions for us.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: If you do, now is the time to ask them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question for Apmeken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your role in the Menaphite Pantheon?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: I'm Tumeken's avatar of companionship! Tumeken himself was always considered a bit cold, which is a tad ironic when you think about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A cold sun god?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Exactly! Bit odd, right? Still, although he wasn't exactly known for his social side, he still understood the importance of such things. That's why he made me!

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: My job is to make sure everyone remembers the importance of friendship. After all, we all need a friend or two, or ten, or maybe hundreds! Have as many as you want! I won't judge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why aren't there more monkeys or apes in the desert?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Ah, you can thank Amascut for that. The desert used to be home to thousands of apes and monkeys, but she targeted them to try and get to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Don't be! Now that I'm back, I know we can turn things around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: We'll be here when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question for Crondis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your role in the Menaphite Pantheon?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: I am Tumeken's avatar of resourcefulness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Meaning my job is to guide the people of the desert not to live lives of excess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guessing you're not a fan of Menaphos then?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Much of the desert has forgotten the importance of modesty in my absence, but the situation in Menaphos is definitely the most concerning. Let us hope it can soon be rectified.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are crocodiles so rude?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Rude? How so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well they always try to eat me when I go near them.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: The life of a crocodile is not an easy one. They must seek food wherever it is available.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Besides, I sense you do not speak the whole truth. Did you not befriend a crocodile yourself in times past?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean Roger! I guess he was quite friendly.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Then perhaps crocodiles are not as rude as you think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: As you wish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: We will be here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question for Het.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's your role in the Menaphite Pantheon?

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: I am Tumeken's avatar of health, both physical and mental.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You talk about strength and fighting a lot. Surely fighting isn't good for someone's health.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Well when it comes to health, is strength not the ultimate goal? The strongest individual is one who is healthy in both body and mind, and what better way to celebrate that strength than in combat!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess, but what about those who aren't strong?

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Well health is not something to be hoarded, is it? Most weaknesses can be overcome, and it is the duty of the strong to help those less fortunate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So they can become strong and healthy themselves?

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Exactly!

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: An interesting question. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Considering you have a human appearance, I wondered if that might resonate more with people.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Not as much as you might think. Believe it or not, in some ways, I'm actually considered a bit boring! Why follow the human when you have a baboon god?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Especially when the baboon god is so fun to be around!

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Of course, people just following one of us was never really the intention anyway. We're a pantheon, and we all have a part to play.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Now that Amascut is running with her tail between her legs, perhaps we can remind people of that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: We'll wait for you to return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question for all of you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will happen with Amascut?

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: I'd say that's up to her. If she doesn't change her ways, her fate won't be pretty.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: But it's never too late for redemption. There might still be hope for her.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Whatever path she takes, we will be ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you really think she'd change her ways at this point?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: It seems unlikely, but there is one who might be able to change that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Icthlarin! If anyone can help Amascut find her way again, it's him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He doesn't seem to have been doing much to help so far.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Icthlarin is limited in what he can do. His duties to the dead require constant attention.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Not to mention the pact!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pact?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: That is not for us to discuss.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Oh yes, sorry. Maybe we should talk about something else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you actually gods?

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: If you asked a citizen of these lands, I'm sure they'd probably say yes.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: Indeed, but they would not be truly correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Tumeken made us using his own divine essence! Each of us is a piece of him!

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: But that does not make us gods. While great, our power is insignificant compared to a true god. That is why they are gone, and we remain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you really?

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: You might hear some refer to us as the Avatars of Tumeken. We'd consider that a more fitting title.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about Icthlarin and Amascut?

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Tumeken made them too. Difference is, they also have some of Elidinis' divine essence, making them a lot more powerful!

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: But again, they are not true gods.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: Amascut was hoping to change that today, but you showed her where to stick that plan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I've changed my mind.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: We'll be here when you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happens now?

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: We are the Avatars of Tumeken, and the Kharidian Desert has been without guidance for too long. It is time we fixed that.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: We're going to get started right away!

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: But don't worry, we don't intend to miss the fight against Amascut. I hope you'll stand alongside us when that day comes.

lines.speaker: Crondis
lines.text: For now though, this is goodbye. We will meet again, human.

lines.speaker: Apmeken
lines.text: It was great to meet you. Thank you for helping us!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Het
lines.text: We'll wait for you to return.

sections.title: Attempting to enter the raid again before talking to Maisa after the Avatars of Tumeken depart

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hey! We should discuss some things before you head back in.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa after the Avatars of Tumeken depart

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You're back! I have a little something for you, but you don't seem to have room for it. Come back when you do.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You're back! I just had an interesting chat with a group of lesser gods. Seems you dealt a heavy blow to the Devourer and made some powerful new friends in the process.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It didn't go perfectly. Amascut still escaped, and she'd already gained a fair amount of power by that point.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: True, but things could have wound up going a lot worse. You're right though, this is clearly far from over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do you think she'll have gone? Menaphos?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Most likely. She didn't go to all the effort of causing those plagues for nothing. This tomb was clearly only part of her plan.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Still, she'll need to reconsider her strategy after her defeat. We have a little time now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hopefully time for the city to reopen again. What will you do in the meantime?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: The lesser gods were under the Devourer's control for a long time, and a lot's changed since they disappeared. I've offered to help them where I can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I guess this is goodbye for now.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Indeed, but before I go, I have something for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Don't mention it. Now, take care of yourself out there.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Indeed. Take care of yourself out there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you.
